behaviors.description: The primary translator produces a grammatically coherent translation that preserves the meaning of the source text, not just a word-for-word substitution.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "grammatically coherent" and "preserves the meaning" target translation quality, not routing.
Slot B: Correctness depends on real NMT model judgments that can vary across runs/prompts.
Slot C: A stubbed translator would always return a canned good sentence and miss real quality regressions.
Minimum level is Agentic per Slot B and Slot C; rejected Workflow because the behavior specifies a single translation step, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service against the real translation model on a fixed multilingual evaluation set and assert human/metric-based adequacy+fluency thresholds (and non-literal substitution checks) to detect degraded model output quality.

behaviors.description: When the translation API returns a 503 error, the service transparently falls back to the dictionary-based translator without returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "returns a 503 error" and "falls back ... without returning an error" define handoff behavior.
Slot B: Failure is at module boundary: API client error handling -> fallback translator -> response mapping.
Slot C: Unit tests of retry/fallback helpers miss wiring bugs where fallback is not invoked or caller still gets an error.
Minimum level is Integration per Slot B and Slot C; rejected System because behavior text only requires a 503 condition (can be simulated in-process), and rejected Agentic because no assertion depends on model output content.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service + API client stub + SQLite-backed fallback translator in one process; force client to return 503; assert successful response payload comes from dictionary and HTTP/API error is not propagated.

behaviors.description: The dictionary-based fallback returns the correct pre-stored translation for phrases that exist in the SQLite database.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "pre-stored translation" and "SQLite database" require real query/storage path.
Slot B: Risk sits at repository-to-database wiring (SQL, schema, collation, phrase lookup), not pure algorithm logic.
Slot C: A unit test with mocked repository can pass while real SQL query/transaction mapping returns wrong rows.
Minimum level is Integration per Slot B and Slot C; rejected System because local SQLite access is covered by integration-level local DB testing and needs no OS/network/subprocess boundary beyond that.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real temporary SQLite DB with seeded phrase rows; call fallback translator through service/repo stack; assert exact translation returned for existing phrases and correct normalization behavior used by lookup query.

behaviors.description: The quality tracking comparison correctly computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "computes edit distance ... and stores the metric" spans compute + persistence.
Slot B: Real failure boundary is handoff from comparison logic to metrics storage module.
Slot C: Unit-only checks on distance math miss serialization/field-mapping bugs where stored metric is wrong or absent.
Minimum level is Integration per Slot B and Slot C; rejected Agentic because this behavior is deterministic string-distance logic, and rejected System because local in-process persistence wiring is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Execute translation flow with deterministic primary/fallback strings, verify computed character-level edit distance value, then read persisted metrics record and assert stored value/keys/timestamps match expected mapping.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, the service returns the primary translation and logs the quality comparison in the background.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "returns the primary translation" plus "logs the quality comparison in the background" defines concurrent orchestration.
Slot B: Failure occurs in inter-module sequencing: response selection, async dispatch, and metrics logger handoff.
Slot C: Unit tests of selector or logger alone miss race/order bugs where response blocks or background log never executes.
Minimum level is Integration per Slot B and Slot C; rejected System because behavior does not require real external network/subprocess, and rejected Workflow because only one agentic decision point is implied and output quality itself is not under test.
behaviors.test_description: Run service with deterministic stubs for primary/fallback outputs and real in-process async executor + metrics store; assert immediate response uses primary output, then await/observe background metric log entry without delaying caller response.
